summary: The Goshen Township Board of Zoning Appeals held a public hearing on two cases. Case BZA 2026-002 was continued to July 20, 2026 at the applicant Rick Royce's request so he could obtain legal representation. Case BZA 2026-003, an area dimensional variance request for 1288 Sandwood Drive, was approved unanimously, reducing the north side yard setback from 20 feet to approximately 10 feet for a one-story garage and primary suite addition to allow aging in place.
decisions: Motion to approve meeting minutes from April 20, 2026 passed unanimously (Groves, Shaw, Theodore)
Motion to continue case BZA 2026-002 to July 20, 2026 at 6:30 PM passed unanimously (Groves, Shaw, Theodore)

Motion to approve area dimensional variance for case BZA 2026-003 reducing north side yard setback from 20 feet to approximately 10 feet at 1288 Sandwood Drive passed unanimously (Groves, Shaw, Theodore)
Motion to adjourn passed unanimously (Groves, Shaw, Theodore)
action_items: Staff to follow up with Brad and Barb Tillotson regarding approved variance for 1288 Sandwood Drive
Rick Royce to obtain legal counsel and prepare case BZA 2026-002 for July 20, 2026 hearing
Staff to prepare case BZA 2026-002 (continued) and Cincinnati United Soccer Club conditional use case (ZC 2026-004) for July 20 meeting
key_amounts: 511 square feet of the 1,419 square foot addition encroaches between the 10-foot and 20-foot setback zone
North side yard setback reduced from 20 feet to approximately 10 feet
28 feet setback on south side, approximately 10 feet on north side, totaling 38 feet of combined side yard (vs. 40 feet required under new standard)
R3 minimum lot area set at 25,000 square feet under new standards
notable_topics: Continuance of case BZA 2026-002 due to applicant requesting legal representation for potential criminal/fine matter
Area dimensional variance for aging-in-place garage and primary suite addition at 1288 Sandwood Drive (BZA 2026-003)
Impact of September 7, 2025 zoning text amendments that doubled R3 side yard setbacks from 10 to 20 feet
Non-conforming lot status created by township-wide text amendments rather than owner actions
Upcoming Cincinnati United Soccer Club conditional use case (ZC 2026-004) at 1456 State Route 28
attendees: Mr. Pruitt (BZA member - absent/not responding to roll call)
Mr. Purcell (BZA member - absent/not responding to roll call)
Mr. Siegler (BZA member - absent/not responding to roll call)
Brian Schwerderman (architect, SMRW Architects, representing Tillotson)
notes: Only three of six BZA members were present (Groves, Shaw, Theodore). The board operated with reduced numbers as noted by the chair. The September 7, 2025 zoning amendments that increased R3 side yard setbacks from 10 to 20 feet created the non-conformity issue for the Tillotson property. The applicant's architect noted they had contacted the township in April 2025 for setback requirements before the amendments took effect and designed accordingly. Significant portions of the transcript are garbled or contain transcription artifacts.
title: BZA 6-15-2026
